proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher baseline ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
Patients with stage_iv = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with stage_iv = 0.
Higher baseline albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear association).
Higher baseline ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
Higher baseline cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
Higher baseline neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association, expected from clinical priors).

analyses.result_summary: Each 1-point increase in ECOG PS reduces PFS by ~1.19 months. Mean PFS: ECOG 0 = 5.27, ECOG 1 = 4.05, ECOG 2 = 2.91 months. Highly significant.
Stage IV patients have ~1.35 months shorter PFS than non-stage-IV (3.70 vs 5.05 months).
Each +1 g/dL albumin associated with +0.47 months PFS.
Higher LDH modestly associated with shorter PFS (beta = -0.000317 per U/L).
Higher baseline CEA associated with shorter PFS (-0.0053 months per ng/mL).
NLR not significantly associated with PFS in univariate model.
Each additional 1% weight loss in past 6 months associated with -0.073 months PFS.
Age has a POSITIVE association with PFS in this cohort (+0.18 months per year), contrary to typical priors. Robust to adjustment for ECOG and stage.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab have longer pfs_months than those who do not (anti-EGFR antibody, expected positive effect).
Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib have longer pfs_months than those who do not.

analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab on PFS=4.285 (n=15078) vs off=4.323 (n=34922); difference -0.038 months. Not significant overall.
Bevacizumab on=4.301 (n=22383) vs off=4.320 (n=27617); difference -0.019. NS.
Pembrolizumab on=4.316 (n=7660) vs off=4.311 (n=42340); difference +0.005. NS.
Encorafenib on=4.316 (n=5111) vs off=4.311; difference +0.005. NS.
Trastuzumab/tucatinib on=4.271 (n=4033) vs off=4.315; difference -0.044. NS.
Regorafenib on=5.089 (n=10022) vs off=4.117 (n=39978); difference +0.972 months. Highly significant -- the only treatment with an unconditional main effect on PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a treatment_cetuximab x kras_mutation interaction such that cetuximab improves pfs_months in KRAS-wildtype but not in KRAS-mutant patients (KRAS-mut interaction effect <= 0).
There is a treatment_cetuximab x nras_mutation interaction such that cetuximab does not benefit NRAS-mutant patients.
There is a treatment_cetuximab x braf_v600e interaction such that cetuximab does not benefit BRAF V600E patients.
There is a treatment_cetuximab x right_sided_primary interaction such that cetuximab benefits left-sided more than right-sided primary tumors.
Restricting to RAS-wildtype, BRAF-wildtype, left-sided patients, treatment_cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term beta=+0.020 (p=0.665). Stratified: KRAS-WT cetux diff=-0.048 (p=0.122); KRAS-MUT cetux diff=-0.028 (p=0.376). No effect of cetuximab in either subgroup; no significant interaction. Hypothesis NOT supported.
NRAS interaction not significant (beta=-0.007, p=0.96). NRAS-WT cetux diff=-0.037, NRAS-MUT diff=-0.044. No benefit in either. Hypothesis NOT supported.
BRAF interaction with cetuximab not significant (beta=-0.026, p=0.805). Hypothesis NOT supported.
Sidedness-cetuximab interaction not significant (beta=+0.026, p=0.583). Left and right both show non-significant negative point estimates for cetuximab. Hypothesis NOT supported.

Even in the canonical anti-EGFR-eligible subgroup (n=16,443), cetuximab on=4.617 vs off=4.684; diff=-0.067 (p=0.125). Cetuximab has no detectable benefit in the dataset's most favorable patient subset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab x msi_high interaction such that pembrolizumab prolongs pfs_months in MSI-high patients but not MSS patients.
MSI-high patients have different baseline pfs_months than MSS patients (direction unspecified).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.002 (p=0.988). Stratified: MSS pembro diff=+0.005 (n_pembro=7264, p=0.861); MSI-H pembro diff=+0.007 (n_pembro=396, p=0.957). Pembrolizumab shows no benefit overall and no enrichment in MSI-H. Hypothesis NOT supported.
PFS: MSI-H=4.293 (n=2513) vs MSS=4.313 (n=47487). Difference -0.019 months, NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a treatment_encorafenib x braf_v600e interaction such that encorafenib prolongs pfs_months in BRAF V600E-mutant patients but not in BRAF-wildtype patients.
BRAF V600E mutation is independently associated with shorter pfs_months (poor-prognosis biomarker).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=-0.141 (p=0.376). Stratified: BRAF-WT enco diff=+0.013 (p=0.718); BRAF V600E enco diff=-0.129 (n_enco=242, p=0.329). No detectable encorafenib benefit in either subgroup. Hypothesis NOT supported.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ braf_v600e + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + age_years + cea_ng_ml + weight_loss_pct_6mo + kras_mutation + right_sided_primary', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: BRAF V600E adjusted effect on PFS = -0.350 months (p=8.5e-65). Strong independent prognostic biomarker for shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib x her2_amplified interaction such that the regimen prolongs pfs_months in HER2-amplified patients but not in HER2-negative patients.
Using her2_amplification (separate column), the same interaction holds.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.063 (p=0.778). HER2- TT diff=-0.046 (p=0.232); HER2+ TT diff=+0.017 (n_TT=120, p=0.934). No benefit detected. Hypothesis NOT supported.
Interaction beta=-0.103 (p=0.647). Same null pattern using the her2_amplification column. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with liver_mets = 1 have shorter pfs_months than those without liver metastases.
Patients with bone_mets = 1 have shorter pfs_months than those without bone metastases.
Patients with pleural_effusion = 1 have shorter pfs_months than those without.
pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories (omnibus comparison).
pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (omnibus comparison; medicaid/uninsured may have shorter PFS).
Rural residence (rural_residence = 1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Female patients (sex_female = 1) have different pfs_months than male patients.
More prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Liver mets effect on PFS = -0.019 months (p=0.516). Not significant; surprisingly null in this cohort. Hypothesis NOT supported.
Bone mets effect = +0.031 (p=0.221), not significant. Hypothesis NOT supported.
Pleural effusion effect = -0.052 (p=0.072), borderline. Hypothesis weakly supported but NS at 0.05.
All race-ethnicity categories vs reference: |beta| <= 0.10, all p > 0.2. No detectable disparity in PFS by race/ethnicity. Hypothesis NOT supported.
Private insurance vs medicaid +0.053 (p=0.094), medicare +0.035 (p=0.27), uninsured -0.028 (p=0.63). No significant differences. Hypothesis NOT supported.
Rural residence +0.025 (p=0.34). NS. Hypothesis NOT supported.
Female sex -0.006 months (p=0.77). No sex difference in PFS. Hypothesis NOT supported.
Prior lines effect +0.008 months (p=0.39). NS. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model of pfs_months that includes ECOG, stage, age, albumin, CEA, weight loss, LDH, NLR, CRP, hemoglobin, all six treatments, key biomarkers, sidedness, and metastatic sites, the established prognostics (ECOG, stage, age, albumin, CEA, weight loss, LDH) and the biomarkers KRAS, BRAF V600E, right-sided primary remain independent predictors, while only treatment_regorafenib retains a positive treatment effect.
kras_mutation is independently prognostic for shorter pfs_months after multivariable adjustment.
right_sided_primary is independently prognostic for shorter pfs_months after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ecog_ps + stage_iv + age_years + albumin_g_dl + cea_ng_ml + weight_loss_pct_6mo + ldh_u_l + nlr + crp_mg_l + hemoglobin_g_dl + treatment_regorafenib + treatment_cetuximab + treatment_bevacizumab + treatment_pembrolizumab + treatment_encorafenib + treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib + kras_mutation + nras_mutation + braf_v600e + msi_high + her2_amplified + right_sided_primary + liver_mets + bone_mets', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable model R^2 = 0.859. Significant independent predictors (p<0.05): ecog_ps (-1.17), stage_iv (-1.36), age_years (+0.18), albumin_g_dl (+0.47), cea_ng_ml (-0.005), weight_loss (-0.076), ldh (-0.0004), treatment_regorafenib (+0.94), kras_mutation (-0.35), braf_v600e (-0.36), right_sided_primary (-0.29). Five other treatments and other biomarkers (NRAS, MSI-H, HER2, liver/bone mets) are not independent predictors. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: Same multivariable model; coefficient on kras_mutation
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted KRAS effect = -0.354 months (p<1e-300). Strong independent poor-prognosis biomarker.

analyses.code: Same multivariable model; coefficient on right_sided_primary
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted right-sided primary effect = -0.287 months (p=1.1e-272). Robust independent poor-prognosis feature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Selected comorbidities (diabetes_mellitus, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, depression_anxiety_diagnosis) are individually associated with shorter pfs_months.
At least one of the 30 SNP markers (snp_rs*) shows an association with pfs_months at the conventional 0.05 significance threshold (top hits to be reported, with the caveat of multiple testing).
The treatment_regorafenib main effect on pfs_months remains significantly positive after adjustment for ECOG, stage IV, age, albumin, weight loss, CEA, KRAS, BRAF V600E, and sidedness.
The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months differs between stage_iv = 1 and stage_iv = 0 (interaction).

analyses.result_summary: diabetes -0.013 (p=0.61), copd +0.006 (p=0.81), CKD +0.032 (p=0.31), heart_failure +0.001 (p=0.98), depression_anxiety -0.052 (p=0.053). Only depression/anxiety borderline; none reach p<0.05. Hypothesis largely NOT supported.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ snp for each of 30 SNPs; rank by p
analyses.result_summary: Top hits at nominal p<0.05: snp_rs1050828 beta=+0.092 (p=0.015); snp_rs1801131 beta=-0.049 (p=0.027); snp_rs1801197 beta=-0.049 (p=0.047). With Bonferroni correction (alpha=0.05/30=0.0017) NONE survive. Likely false positives. Hypothesis only nominally supported.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib + ecog_ps + stage_iv + age_years + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + cea_ng_ml + kras_mutation + braf_v600e + right_sided_primary', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted regorafenib effect = +0.943 months (p<1e-300). Robust to extensive case-mix adjustment. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Interaction beta=+0.070 (p=0.151). Stratified: stage<IV diff=+0.941, stage IV diff=+1.011. Both subgroups benefit similarly; no significant interaction. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib x kras_mutation interaction: regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated in KRAS-wildtype patients and is largely absent in KRAS-mutant patients.
There is a treatment_regorafenib x braf_v600e interaction: regorafenib's PFS benefit is largely absent in BRAF V600E-mutant patients.
There is a treatment_regorafenib x right_sided_primary interaction: regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated in left-sided primary tumors and absent in right-sided primaries.
Among patients who are KRAS-wildtype AND BRAF-wildtype, regorafenib produces a markedly larger PFS gain than in patients with either mutation.
There is a treatment_regorafenib x ecog_ps interaction (ECOG modifies the regorafenib benefit).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -1.629 months (p=7.7e-226). Stratified: KRAS-WT regorafenib on=5.77 vs off=4.12 (diff +1.66, p=6e-293, n_on=5816); KRAS-MUT regorafenib on=4.14 vs off=4.12 (diff +0.027, p=0.46, n_on=4206). Regorafenib's PFS benefit is essentially confined to KRAS-wildtype patients. Hypothesis STRONGLY SUPPORTED.

Interaction beta = -1.183 (p=6.1e-23). Stratified: BRAF-WT regorafenib diff=+1.03 (p=5e-226); BRAF V600E regorafenib diff=-0.16 (n_on=468, p=0.13). Regorafenib does not benefit BRAF V600E patients. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

Interaction beta = -1.454 (p=4.1e-168). Stratified: left-sided regorafenib diff=+1.48 (n_on=6524, p=4e-274); right-sided regorafenib diff=+0.026 (n_on=3498, p=0.52). Regorafenib's benefit is confined to left-sided primaries. Hypothesis STRONGLY SUPPORTED.

KRAS-WT & BRAF-WT (n=26830): regorafenib on=5.93 vs off=4.12, diff=+1.81 months. KRAS-MUT or BRAF-MUT (n=23170): on=4.13 vs off=4.12, diff=+0.009. The benefit is essentially zero unless both genes are wildtype. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib * ecog_ps', data=df).fit() and smf.ols including this interaction in adjusted model
analyses.result_summary: Univariate interaction beta=+0.005 (p=0.89); subgroup diffs ECOG 0/1/2 = +0.93/+1.00/+0.90 (all p<<0.001). In a multivariable model with prognostics, the interaction term becomes -0.051 (p=0.0003), suggesting modestly diminished benefit at higher ECOG once case mix is controlled, but the magnitude is small relative to the main effect (+0.96). Hypothesis WEAKLY SUPPORTED.
